item.title: Jotbook 1.04
item.description:
# Jotbook 1.04
## New - **Automatic updates** — Jotbook now ships with Sparkle. It checks for a new release on launch and once a day after that, and installs updates silently the next time you quit and reopen the app. No more right-click → Open dance for future updates. - **About section in Settings** — a new **About** panel shows the current version and build, toggles automatic update checks, offers a **Check for Updates…** button, and links out to the full release history on GitHub. - **Check for Updates… menu item** — added to the menubar right-click menu so you can pull the latest release on demand without opening Settings.
## Install Download `Jotbook-1.04.dmg`, open it, drag Jotbook to Applications. First launch: right-click → Open, then System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway (authenticate with Touch ID), then Open in the final dialog.

item.title: Jotbook 1.05
item.description:
# Jotbook 1.05
## New
- **Editable preview mode.** The preview window can now double as a lightweight editor for your notes file. Turn it on to edit the raw markdown in place, with a formatting toolbar down the left (bold, italic, strikethrough, inline code, link, H1/H2/H3, bullet, numbered, blockquote) and a quick-insert bar across the top populated from your snippet list (TODO:, Idea:, ?:, or whatever you've customized). Standard macOS text shortcuts — copy, paste, undo/redo, word-wise navigation, select-all, right-click menu — all work as you'd expect. - **Edit toggle in the preview title bar.** A small "Edit" button in the top-right of the preview window flips between rendered view and editor mode without having to open Settings. It stays in sync with the Settings toggle. - **Editable preview setting.** Under Settings › Preview window, a new toggle — *Make preview editable (show formatting toolbars)* — controls whether the preview opens as an editor or as the rendered markdown. Off by default, so upgrading changes nothing unless you turn it on. - **Safe save/reload behavior.** Edits are written to disk automatically when the preview loses focus or closes; if you add a new entry from the capture popover while the preview was blurred, the preview reloads the latest file contents when you return to it, so your edits and your captures don't fight each other.
## Install & update
- **Already installed?** Jotbook auto-updates — you'll get a prompt shortly, or pick *Check for Updates…* in Settings › About to grab it now. - **First time?** Download `Jotbook-1.05.dmg`, open it, drag Jotbook to Applications. First launch: right-click → Open, then System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway (authenticate with Touch ID), then Open in the final dialog.

item.title: Jotbook 1.06
item.description:
# Jotbook 1.06
## New
- **Notch-aware capture panel.** On 14" and 16" MacBook Pros with a notch, the capture panel now emerges from the notch itself with a spring animation — concave shoulders that tuck into the notch, a black-to-editor gradient that fades cleanly into the body, and a drop shadow that hugs the live shape. Reads as part of the Mac, not a floating popup. - **Opens on the screen you're looking at.** Multi-monitor setups now open the capture panel on whichever display has your mouse cursor, instead of always anchoring to a fixed screen. - **Works over full-screen apps.** When the menubar is auto-hidden (full-screen app), the panel collapses its top black region so you don't see a tall dark band above the editor. - **Fade-in animation** on open for a smoother appearance. - **Reduce Motion support.** With Reduce Motion enabled in System Settings › Accessibility › Display, Jotbook falls back to a simpler slide-and-fade animation instead of the notch spring.
## Fixed
- **Capture panel now appears correctly when the Jotbook menubar icon is hidden by Bartender** (or any other menubar manager). Previously, a hidden icon meant the panel anchored to off-screen coordinates and landed in a screen corner. The panel now anchors to the notch (or the screen-top center on non-notched displays) regardless of whether the status icon is visible.
## Install & update
- **Already installed?** Jotbook auto-updates — you'll get a prompt shortly, or pick *Check for Updates…* in Settings › About to grab it now. - **First time?** Download `Jotbook-1.06.dmg`, open it, drag Jotbook to Applications. First launch: right-click → Open, then System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway (authenticate with Touch ID), then Open in the final dialog.

item.title: Jotbook 1.07
item.description:
# Jotbook 1.07
## New
- **⌘F search in the preview window.** Press ⌘F with the preview open — in *either* rendered-markdown mode or editable-text mode — and a dark floating search overlay appears, matching the capture panel's search one-for-one. Type a query, click a result to scroll the preview to that line and highlight it natively. The overlay stays open so you can page through multiple matches; Esc or click anywhere outside to dismiss. In editable mode, the search reads the live buffer, so unsaved edits are findable too. - **Search by date.** Typing a date like `2026-04-24` (or any substring of a `###` timestamp header) now surfaces every entry filed under that header — in both the capture panel's ⌘F and the new preview search. Regular text matching still works the same way; date search is additive. The empty-state hint under the search field explains this.
## Changed
- **App-wide dark theme.** The preview window, settings window, and capture panel now consistently use a pure-black background with dark-styled controls. Previously the preview and settings windows followed system appearance; now they match the always-dark notch-blend capture panel so the whole app reads as one surface. - **Capture panel is flat solid black.** Removed the black-to-grey collar/fade band that used to sit between the notch and the editor area. The shell is one continuous surface now — a cleaner blend with notched MacBooks, and no visible edge when the panel overlays a dark full-screen app. - **Settings window polish.** - Full dark/black treatment. - Brighter divider lines inside each section card, easier to read against the dark surface. - In the middle column's *Preview window* section, the Auto-refresh and Make-editable toggles now live in their own card, separate from the keyboard-shortcut row. - **Preview window text-editor mode** now explicitly renders on a black surface with white text, matching the rest of the app instead of inheriting a lighter system text background.
## Install & update
- **Already installed?** Jotbook auto-updates — you'll get a prompt shortly, or pick *Check for Updates…* in Settings › About to grab it now. - **First time?** Download `Jotbook-1.07.dmg`, open it, drag Jotbook to Applications. First launch: right-click → Open, then System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway (authenticate with Touch ID), then Open in the final dialog.

item.title: Jotbook 1.08
item.description:
# Jotbook 1.08
A small maintenance release. No new features; one minor cosmetic tweak.
## Changed
- **Settings › About** now shows the version as just `Version 1.08`, dropping the redundant build-number suffix that previously read `Version 1.08 (1.08)`.
## Install & update
- **Already installed?** Jotbook auto-updates — you'll get a prompt shortly, or pick *Check for Updates…* in Settings › About to grab it now. - **First time?** Download `Jotbook-1.08.dmg`, open it, drag Jotbook to Applications. First launch: right-click → Open, then System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway (authenticate with Touch ID), then Open in the final dialog.
